global_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Open-source feature comparison matrix of 77 memory systems for AI coding agents, mapping 79 features across data models, retrieval, knowledge lifecycle, extraction, architecture and benchmarks with source-backed evidence for each claim.

MCP server that indexes codebases into a persistent knowledge graph so AI agents avoid re-reading entire projects every session; signals shift from bigger context windows (2024) and retrieval (2025) to long-term agent memory (2026).

2.7k-star open-source GitHub library with 985 commits containing full runnable notebooks for multi-agent orchestration (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.), persistent memory (Mem0, hierarchical FAISS+SQLite), reasoning loops, MCP servers, A2A protocols and agentic RAG.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Tool-call interceptor for LangChain/LangGraph agents that enforces delegation scope and limits while emitting signed proof artifacts; adds verifiable guardrails without changing existing agent code.
near_misses.reason: Practical agent tooling but lower engagement and narrower scope than memory/architecture repos above.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Agent capable of one-shot generation of complex 3D games, full SaaS/mobile/desktop apps and workflow automation with free hosting/compute; promotes smart routing across LLMs (mixture-of-agents).
near_misses.reason: Promotional product demo from seed expert rather than new open-source innovation or benchmark.

notes: Seed experts yielded only casual/promotional posts in the 24h window; no high-signal technical discoveries. Global search surfaced three strong memory-and-agent-infrastructure items meeting all filtering criteria. Additional agent-tooling posts placed in near_misses due to lower signal or promotional nature.
